title: senna durangensis
intro: Senna durangensis, also known as Mexican senna, is a perennial shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a purgative and laxative, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides such as emodin and rhein. The health benefits of Senna durangensis include relief from constipation, hemorrhoids, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve digestion. Senna durangensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be found in dried leaf form, which can be consumed after being steeped in hot water.
